context.description: A philosophical discussion contrasts dream experience with waking life to argue that dreams normalize absurdity and that memory can be used as a measure of consciousness. It uses a thought experiment about a dream visitor asking about a tire-change appointment and a shopping list to show how dreams lack temporal context.
normalization_notes: Classified as body content because the page contains continuous philosophical prose.

rows.paragraph_text: Dreams make absurd events feel natural, with no surprise at flying, unstable matter, or impossible movement through strange architectures.
The text proposes recall after waking as a measure of what awareness includes and as a way to gauge the height of consciousness.

A thought experiment shows that a dreamer would likely absorb a visitor into the dream and would struggle to understand tomorrow, appointments, or a shopping list because dreams lack ordinary temporal context.

content.free_text: book_page book_body_content theoretical_discussion Book page on dream perception and consciousness. It argues that dream experience normalizes absurdity and uses recall as a measure of awareness. A thought experiment explains how dreams lack temporal context for appointments and shopping lists.
